# Loyalty Interchange Protocol (LIP)
# Compact index for AI coding agents. When docs and spec disagree, spec/ wins.

> LIP is a vendor-neutral loyalty transaction protocol for restaurant, QSR, and
> franchise ordering. It is NOT a customer auth platform — apps use a BFF that
> holds the merchant API key and maps users to member_id.

## Quick commands

npm install && npm start
npm run lip -- doctor http://127.0.0.1:3210 --api-key lip-dev-key
npm run lip -- test http://127.0.0.1:3210 --api-key lip-dev-key
npm run example:sdk
npm run test

## Agent setup

npx skills add .
npm run mcp          # official MCP server (stdio)
mcp.json             # Cursor MCP config

Agent docs:
- docs/using-lip-with-ai.md
- docs/ai-prompts.md
- skills/README.md

## Agent docs

- docs/using-lip-with-ai.md — start here for LLM-assisted development
- docs/ai-prompts.md — copy/paste prompts for common integration tasks
- docs/getting-started.md — shortest path to a working request
- docs/typescript-sdk.md — LipClient, idempotency, webhooks, order builder
- docs/api-endpoints.md — HTTP operations and curl examples
- docs/webhook-delivery.md — signed CloudEvents and receiver setup
- docs/reference-platform.md — server, Admin, SQLite sandbox

## Normative spec (read before changing behavior)

- spec/lifecycle.md — enroll, evaluate, accrue, reserve, capture, reverse, adjust
- spec/core.md — protocol context, idempotency, problem details
- spec/profiles/foodservice.md — FoodserviceOrder, earning exclusions, channels
- spec/webhooks.md — HMAC-SHA256 signature profile, deduplication
- spec/openapi.yaml — generated HTTP binding (14 operations)
- spec/schemas/ — JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 payloads

## Runnable examples

- examples/typescript/full-lifecycle.ts — enroll → evaluate → accrue → reserve → capture → reverse → adjust

## SDK (prefer over raw fetch)

import { LipClient } from "@loyalty-interchange/sdk"

Operations:
  lip.members.enroll / lookup
  lip.orders.evaluate / adjust
  lip.accruals.post
  lip.redemptions.reserve / capture / reverse
  lip.accounts.get / lip.ledger.list / lip.ledger.adjust / lip.programs.get

Idempotency: derive keys from business ids (order_id), not random UUIDs per retry.
  lip.accruals.post(body, { idempotencyKey: `accrual:${order.order_id}` })

Webhooks: verifyWebhook(payload, secret, timestamp, signature) before JSON.parse

## HTTP API (Bearer merchant key)

POST /lip/v1/members/enroll
POST /lip/v1/members/lookup
POST /lip/v1/orders/evaluate
POST /lip/v1/accruals
POST /lip/v1/redemptions/reserve
POST /lip/v1/redemptions/capture
POST /lip/v1/redemptions/reverse
POST /lip/v1/orders/adjust
POST /lip/v1/accounts/get
POST /lip/v1/ledger/list
POST /lip/v1/ledger/manual-adjustments
POST /lip/v1/programs/get

Discovery: GET /.well-known/lip, GET /health
Operations: GET /metrics (Bearer auth), RateLimit-* headers, JSON request logs
Admin: http://127.0.0.1:3210/admin/ (same key as API)

## Checkout lifecycle (foodservice)

1. orders/evaluate — preview earn + redeem eligibility (before payment)
2. redemptions/reserve — hold points for a reward (optional)
3. [payment provider]
4. accruals — post earned points
5. redemptions/capture — finalize redemption (if reserved)
On failure after reserve: redemptions/reverse
On refund: redemptions/reverse (if redeemed) + orders/adjust (claw back earn)

## Webhook events (reference platform)

org.loyalty-interchange.member.enrolled.v1
org.loyalty-interchange.order.accrued.v1
org.loyalty-interchange.order.adjusted.v1
org.loyalty-interchange.redemption.reserved.v1
org.loyalty-interchange.redemption.captured.v1
org.loyalty-interchange.redemption.reversed.v1

Enable: LIP_WEBHOOK_URL + LIP_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars on lip serve
Failed deliveries persist in the SQLite outbox and resume after restart.

## Implementation map (change behavior here)

- packages/protocol/src — schema source, generated types
- packages/reference/src/engine.ts — loyalty engine rules
- packages/server/src/server.ts — HTTP routes
- packages/sdk/src/client.ts — idiomatic TypeScript client
- packages/cli/src/cli.ts — lip CLI (serve, doctor, test, validate)
- apps/admin/src/App.tsx — reference Admin dashboard

## BFF integration pattern (customer apps)

App holds session token only. BFF holds LIP_API_KEY.
BFF enrolls on signup, calls evaluate for cart preview, runs reserve→pay→accrue→capture.
Reference: sakura-japan repo (Expo app + server/index.mjs BFF).

## Not in LIP (build in your app or wait for platform)

- Customer auth (OTP, social, sessions)
- Campaigns, segments, push, CRM, analytics
- Runtime program editing (use --program at boot)
- Live loyalty operations over MCP (official MCP supplies docs, schemas, and validation only)

## Maintainer

PLAN.md — roadmap
CONTRIBUTING.md — conformance and compatibility rules
npm run generate — regen OpenAPI + schemas from protocol source
npm run verify — typecheck + unit tests
